channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.ign.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.ign.com

item.title: Minecraft Dungeons 2 Revealed With Fall 2026 Launch
item.content:encoded:
Mojang Studios has officially announced that Minecraft Dungeons 2 is in development with plans to launch this fall.

The developer behind the ever-evolving sandbox phenomenon took to Minecraft Live March 2026 to announce that its top-down dungeon-crawling spinoff is finally getting a sequel for PC, Nintendo Switch, Switch 2, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X | S. While a release date was not announced, eager players can add it to their wishlists starting today.

"A hero’s work is never done!" an official description on its Steam page says. "Disorder is brewing, and the world stands before a threat unlike any other. A new danger is growing in power, ready to descend upon the land and unleash mayhem.

"Return to the world of Minecraft Dungeons in an all‑new action RPG adventure, brimming with high‑stakes encounters, thrilling challenges, and never‑before‑seen locations, as you set out to save a world in crisis. Will you and your allies prevail against the forces of evil and save the day once more?"

The original Minecraft Dungeons delivered an action-focused twist for the Minecraft universe when it launched for PC, Nintendo Switch, PlayStation 4, and Xbox One in May 2020. Although it only managed to land in 15th place for that month’s top-selling games, players gravitated to its Diablo-like gameplay over time.

Following a number of free updates, including one that added crossplay, and multiple expansion packs, Mojang ended development on Minecraft Dungeons in 2023. In a note to fans at the time, the studio declared that the project had “exceeded our wildest dreams” after reaching 25 million players.

Minecraft Dungeons 2 Minecraft Live Trailer - Screenshots

Today’s Minecraft Dungeons 2 announcement only offers a tease for how Mojang will expand on this corner of its blocky universe. For now, it’s unclear when additional updates, including a first look at gameplay, will arrive, but with its release date planned for sometime in the near future, it shouldn’t be too long before Mojang has more to share.

Minecraft Dungeons 2 will release at an unspecified point this fall. Until then, you can see what we thought of the original game in our 7/10 review. You can also check out everything announced during the March 2026 Minecraft Live 2026 presentation.

Michael Cripe is a freelance writer with IGN. He's best known for his work at sites like The Pitch, The Escapist, and OnlySP. Be sure to give him a follow on Bluesky (@mikecripe.bsky.social) and Twitter (@MikeCripe).

Recommends

In This Article



https://www.ign.com/articles/minecraft-dungeons-2-revealed-with-fall-2026-launch
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Mojang Reveals Chaos Cubed Update Coming to Minecraft Later This Year With Tiny Takeover Release Date Set for Next Week
item.content:encoded:
Mojang Studios has unveiled more information about updates coming to Minecraft in 2026, including the release date for Tiny Takeover and a sneak peek at the Chaos Cubed game drop.

The Minecraft Live March 2026 presentation served as the backdrop for all of the developer’s latest announcements today. Alongside glimpses at how it will expand the wider Minecraft universe, the show also came with more information about how players can expect the base experience to change in the coming months.

Tiny Takeover

It all starts with Tiny Takeover, which is now set to launch with a March 24 release date. Players were previously told to expect new and improved versions of existing baby mobs, but today’s livestream showed off more from the adorable revision taking over the Overworld.

While tiny versions of mobs have existed for years, Tiny Takeover gives almost every critter a makeover with new looks, fresh sound effects, and even the option to keep a mob as a baby forever, thanks to an item called the Golden Dandelion. Farm mobs, such as pigs, cows, cats, and chickens, will also now spawn with what Mojang describes as “unique sound personalities.” Players will be able to spawn baby mobs using spawn eggs, too, with craftable name tags and the new trumpet sound effect also included in the update.

Minecraft Tiny Takeover Screenshots - Minecraft Live March 2026

Almost every baby mob has been touched up, with the full list including new models for dozens of creatures. As shared by Mojang, you can see a list of mobs benefiting from the update below:

o Baby farm mobs: puppies, kittens, piglets, calves, chicks, lambs, ocelots, and bunnies

o Baby mounts: donkeys, mules, and every horse variant

o Aquatic baby mobs: axolotls, dolphins, squids, and turtles

o Hostile and scary baby mobs: zombies, zombified piglins, hoglins, zoglins, zombie villagers, husks, gurgles, piglins, and striders

o Baby villagers: all seven variants

o Wild baby mobs: foxes, polar bears, pandas, bees, llamas, armadillos, goats, and snifflets

Chaos Cubed

The Tiny Takeover section of Minecraft Live pulled back the curtain on features fans were told to expect earlier this month. Chaos Cubed, however, is an all-new update that will arrive at an unannounced point later in 2026.

Get ready for the first look at Chaos Cubed!

New sulfur caves are calling… but don’t get too comfortable. Step too close and your world might start to warp.

Plus, meet the sulfur cube, a mob that evolves based on what it absorbs. Wood, ice, and beyond… the possibilities are… pic.twitter.com/Q7QGqn2oey

— Minecraft (@Minecraft) March 21, 2026

Where the March game drop focuses on mobs, Chaos Cubed aims to change how players look at the world around them. Part of this includes the new sulfur cube, which changes properties by absorbing materials. The extent of the new block’s abilities has yet to be revealed, but Mojang teases that it will be able to alter its abilities by absorbing surrounding materials. Players will also be able to feed the sulfur cube.

The sulfur cube can be found in the new, aptly named sulfur cave, which has been added to the Overworld’s pool of existing cave layouts. These yellow and red, toxic areas come with sulfur pools and other new blocks, with the former featuring gas particles that can trigger a noxious effect.

Minecraft Chaos Cubed Screenshots - Minecraft Live March 2026

Mojang is changing Minecraft one block at a time as it attempts to keep longtime players coming back with new mobs to meet and caves to uncover. For more, you can see everything announced during the March 2026 Minecraft Live show. You can see the latest casting news for the upcoming A Minecraft Movie sequel.

Michael Cripe is a freelance writer with IGN. He's best known for his work at sites like The Pitch, The Escapist, and OnlySP. Be sure to give him a follow on Bluesky (@mikecripe.bsky.social) and Twitter (@MikeCripe).



https://www.ign.com/articles/mojang-reveals-chaos-cubed-update-coming-to-minecraft-later-this-year-with-tiny-takeover-release-date-set-for-next-week
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Minecraft World Concept Art Reveals New Theme Park Coming in 2027
item.content:encoded:
They're gonna need a lot of redstone.

Minecraft World, a theme park based on the video game from Mojang Studios, will officially bring Creepers and Crafting Tables to the real world when it opens its doors in 2027.

Mojang Studios and Merlin Entertainments announced their plans for a $70 million project based on a gaming titan during Minecraft Live March 2026 today. Although plans for its debut at Chessington World of Adventures in Greater London are still coming together, future attendees are told to expect a park inspired by iconic biomes, mobs, items, and more.

You can see concept art for Minecraft World, which features wooden houses, Creepers, TNT blocks, and even a Nether dining area, below.

Minecraft World Concept Art - Minecraft Live March 2026

“Minecraft World represents a meaningful milestone in our ongoing journey to expand the Minecraft universe,” senior creative director of entertainment at Minecraft, Torfi Frans Ólafsson, said in a statement. “We're thrilled to have partnered with Merlin Entertainments to realize a place where you can literally be in the Minecraft Overworld and have an adventure of your own with your family and friends. The team at Merlin Entertainments and Mojang have worked hard to craft an experience that feels immersive, authentic and welcoming, and we can't wait for our community to experience it.”

Mojang and Merlin tell fans to look forward to “a thrilling world first coaster, interactive adventures, epic block built playscapes, and themed retail and dining.” Like other IP-based theme parks before it, Minecraft World will pull from the universe it’s inspired by for the attractions, though additional details remain under wraps.

Minecraft World will be the first major park inspired by Mojang’s video game phenomenon, but theme parks based on video games aren’t exactly new. Nintendo is the obvious example, having already built attractions based on Super Mario Bros. and Donkey Kong for Super Nintendo World parks across the globe. Universal Studios has also previously launched haunted houses based on the Five Nights at Freddy’s, Fallout, and The Last of Us for Halloween Horror Nights in the past.

Mojang and Merlin are still busy building Minecraft World for its 2027 grand opening. It’s unclear when we’ll get to see the park or learn more about its attractions, for now. In the meantime, you can read up on everything else announced at Minecraft Live 2026.

Michael Cripe is a freelance writer with IGN. He's best known for his work at sites like The Pitch, The Escapist, and OnlySP. Be sure to give him a follow on Bluesky (@mikecripe.bsky.social) and Twitter (@MikeCripe).

Recommends

In This Article

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
